summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Fed’s top payments official to exit
articles.description: Mark Gould, who was the Federal Reserve’s first payments chief, said he will retire this year, but stay on while the central bank seeks his successor.

articles.title: Adyen CFO heads for exit
articles.description: The chief financial officer departure comes as the Dutch payments processor seeks to build up its U.S. presence.

articles.title: Trump should nix high-cost loans
articles.description: “If the president wants to provide people relief from high-interest debt ... he should lead a crackdown against high-cost credit schemes,” writes a consumer advocate lawyer.

articles.title: Bill shuffles senior executives
articles.description: Business payments company Bill Holdings named new leaders for product, technology and revenue roles amid a broader restructuring.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard envision agentic commerce benefits
articles.description: Taking humans out of purchases is likely to multiply transactions and thereby boost fees, chief financial officers of the card networks said.

articles.title: Tennessee levies tax on remittances
articles.description: The Volunteer State will add a $10 tax for cross-border payments, and an extra 2% fee for some, under a new law drawing the ire of payments players.

articles.title: FIS, banks mull network uses
articles.description: The payments processor is in talks with banks about possible new strategies for use of its card network.

articles.title: OCC aims to derail state card law
articles.description: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency urged a judge Friday to block an Illinois law on interchange fees that the agency has preempted.
